command_injection.description: OS command execution — immediate RCE if attacker-controlled

buffer_overflow_copy.description: Unbounded copy — overflow if source is attacker-controlled and longer than destination

memory_copy_sized.description: Sized copy — overflow if size parameter is attacker-controlled or miscalculated
memory_copy_sized.note: snprintf/strncpy are safer but still dangerous if size comes from attacker

format_string.description: Format string — dangerous ONLY if format string itself is attacker-controlled
format_string.note: printf(user_input) is vuln. printf("%s", user_input) is safe.

path_traversal.description: File operations — path traversal if path is attacker-controlled without validation

sql_injection.description: SQL execution — injection if query contains attacker-controlled strings

heap_management.description: Heap free — UAF/double-free if pointer lifecycle is mismanaged
heap_management.note: Look for: free then use, double free, free of stack/global pointer

pool_allocation.description: Allocation — integer overflow in size calculation leads to undersized buffer

type_confusion.description: Type confusion — wrong type interpretation of attacker-provided data

privilege_operations.description: Privilege manipulation — escalation if attacker can influence parameters

kernel_write.description: Kernel write-to-user or memory mapping — info leak or arbitrary write
